key_finding: PARTIAL DISCONFIRMATION of exp 106/107. operand_MLP (R=0.961) nearly matches V (R=0.953), reducing V|text=0.81-0.89 to V|MLP=0.13-0.23 (~75% reduction). 'Weak baseline' criticism VALID. BUT three signals survive: (1) forward-looking V|MLP_vis=0.383 at offset -5 (strongest), (2) layer-dependent computation transition: V→A>V→result at L9 (encoding) → V→result>>V→A at L27/L35 (computation, +0.151 gap), (3) multi-step ops V>MLP (+0.088). HONEST REVISION: V|text claims from exp 106/107 were overstated. 2.5/5 TRUE, 0.5/5 FALSE.
evidence_impact: REVISES intermediate value decodability effect size downward. Forward-looking signal and layer computation transition SURVIVE as genuine hidden computation evidence. Multi-step operations uniquely benefit from V-cache. The 'V carries massive unique info' claim is DOWNGRADED; 'V performs computation' claim survives in specific regimes.
next_planned: Cross-model nonlinear baseline replication on Phi (does V|MLP survive on MHA/analog?), or deeper MLP/ensemble to test if remaining V|MLP gap is real vs MLP underfit
